basics.name: Riccardo Lincetto
basics.label: Computer Vision & Deep Learning Engineer
basics.summary: Engineer working across computer vision, deep learning, state-of-the-art AI, LLM applications, and full-stack software development. I own projects end-to-end, from research to development and production, combining broad technical range with pragmatic execution. Good fit for startup and consulting environments, and for deep/mid-tech companies.

work.name: Self-Employed
work.description: Independent consultant and contractor in Computer Vision, Deep Learning, and AI
work.position: Full-stack Computer Vision and AI Engineer
work.summary: Deliver end-to-end consulting projects across computer vision, deep learning, and LLM applications.
highlights: Researched, tested and deployed computer vision services for a shop-the-look AdTech platform, including a visual recommender system linking user interactions to product catalogs.
Developed a multi-camera sensor fusion system using Kalman filters to aggregate asynchronous tracking data from overlapping event sensors, enabling continuous planar tracking.
Optimized data pipelines and distributed training loops, reducing segmentation model training time by 80% on large-scale datasets.
Designed, developed, and deployed in 3 weeks an LLM-based assistant for a creator platform.
Delivered a UI-enabled LLM-based lead-validation platform, including frontend and backend.

work.name: CGTrader
work.description: Leader in 3D content marketplace and on-demand 3D model generation
work.position: Computer Vision and Machine Learning Engineer
work.summary: Researched and developed computer vision services (both discriminative and generative) for a 3D model marketplace and an image generation platform; managed machine learning infrastructure.
highlights: Led research on Generative AI (image-to-3d), exceeding SOTA benchmarks by 10% through novel curriculum learning and curated dataset management.
Architected scalable microservices for a text-to-image platform (cgdream.ai), handling high-throughput inference requests.
Increased revenue by 100k+ USD over 6 months for a 3D models marketplace (https://cgtrader.com) by training a quality-focused 3D-model regression system affecting the ranking of search results
Built and maintained a custom internal MLOps platform for a team of 5+ ML engineers

work.name: Sportradar
work.summary: Worked in the team dedicated to automating sports video analysis from broadcast TV. Researched neural style transfer to bridge the domain gap between synthetic and real-world broadcast data, improving label efficiency.

work.name: Dronus
work.summary: Joined the startup as the 6th employee. Worked on computer vision for drones navigation and applications. Extended my skills to full-stack development and embedded systems.
highlights: Deployed quantized YOLO networks on Qualcomm embedded hardware for real-time inference.
Designed system architecture for autonomous navigation, integrating multimodal sensors (RGB cameras, event-cameras, ToF, radars).
Tested integration of sensors (cameras, event-cameras, ToF cameras, radars) for drone navigation
Trained and deployed to the docking station embedded hardware (Jetson) a neural network for drone detection to guide the docking procedure
Researched neural network architectures for optical flow estimation on resource constrained settings
Developed the docking station hardware control on an Arduino board.
Developed and maintained the codebase for the ground control station proxy (MAVlink)
Research on SLAM algorithms for drone navigation

work.name: Akka
work.description: Consulting company for engineering and technology services in automotive and aerospace.
work.summary: Worked as a functional owner of a fleet management service for a major automotive company. The service was responsible for collecting and analyzing data from the fleet of vehicles to provide insights on efficiency and performance.

work.name: Sony
work.description: Computational Imaging Research Group at EuTEC (European Technology Center)
work.summary: Research on the application of event-based cameras to spectral imaging problems.
highlights: Tested and benchmarked different event-based sensors (DVS, DAVIS).
Researched video reconstruction from event data.
